mj_eu.title: Morning Juice – Europe Session Prep
mj_eu.summary:
• Trump told aides to prepare for an extended US Naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.
• US looks to intensify economic pressure on Iran as the war enters its third month.
• Australia’s inflation remained above RBA’s 2-3% target due to higher fuel costs.
• Elevated price pressures keep Australian policymakers on a tightening path.
• UAE’s shock decision to quit OPEC blindsided partners, cartel fights for relevance.
• UK could slip into recession, forced to hike rates if Middle East crisis worsens.

wrap.title: Tech Selloff Hits Stocks as AI Doubts Resurface – US Market Wrap
wrap.summary:
• Stocks fell, yields rose, oil climbed above $111, and the dollar was steady.
• Technology giants' selloff dragged equities lower amid AI investment return concerns.
• S&P 500 pulled back from a record, Nasdaq 100 fell 1% ahead of key earnings.
• Brent rose above $111 due to fears of a prolonged peace process closing Strait of Hormuz.
• Fed expected to hold rates for a third meeting due to inflation and resilient labor market.
• JPMorgan CEO Dimon warned of a potential credit downturn despite strong bank earnings.

mj_us.title: Morning Juice – US Session Prep (29th April)
mj_us.summary:
• Treasuries fluctuated, oil prices rose, and the dollar edged higher in early trading.
• Brent crude climbed above $115 per barrel, reaching a four-week high.
• Market sentiment is ahead of the Fed’s April rate decision today.
• Key economic data includes US Durable Goods and Weekly EIA Crude Oil Inventories.
• BoC Rate Decision and Statement are also scheduled for today.
• Major tech earnings from Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Qualcomm are expected.

dockets.title: US Durable Goods for March Prelim
